steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The contrast word but marks the turning point.

Faggio's research shows that “certain factors influence some industry groupings more strongly than others“—meaning the strength of these factors varies across sectors.

The economists must have assumed something different.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Consistent expresses the assumption that reasons for clustering are the same across all sectors—exactly what economists believed before Faggio contradicted them. Variable would describe what Faggio actually found (varying strength), not what economists incorrectly assumed; combined and artificial don't fit the context.

This confirms C is correct.
